docs.title: Overview
docs.text: We are fixing a few details in the docs. If you find any irregularities or mistakes, please be patient with us. Thank you for understanding :)

docs.title: QVEX Lynepad: Macro keypad with 11 keys, a rotary joystick, and a thumb wheel.
Description
docs.text: QVEX Lynepad is an additional, fully programmable keyboard suitable for any kind of design work including photo editing, video editing, PCB design, 3D modeling and so much more. It allows you to press shortcuts or run complex macros using a single key.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 11 Gateron MX green keys (80g force, clicky) (hot-swappable in rev.2.0 or newer)
  • 5-axis wheel (rotation and 4-way tilt + push)
  • Thumb wheel (rotation + push)
  • Ergonomic and minimalistic design
  • White backlighting (rev.2.0 or newer)
  • 4 programmable RGB LEDs
  • Power indication LED
  • Anti-slip silicone pads (only available with the enclosure option).
  • Prepared mapping for KiCad
  • A total of 21 physical actions (pressing button, rotating wheel etc.) and up to 54 actions using combinations.

docs.title: Docs content
docs.text:
• Quickstart guide - get started using Lynepad in a few minutes
  • Hardware - explaining how the hardware is designed, so you can use it with our firmware
  • Firmware - explaining how the pre-loaded firmware works
  • Programming - get started with programming your own firmware
  • Resources - everything you may find useful

docs.title: Available firmwares
docs.text:
What is firmware?

Firmware is the program that runs inside of the Lynepad. It is responsible for detecting key-presses, and communicating with the connected computer. Different firmwares can behave differently, and support communication with a different software on your computer. You can replace the firmware by uploading (flashing) another.

New default firmware

The QMK firmware has become a default firmware starting from order #00007 on lynepad.com store.

QMK Firmware (Default) QVEX Firmware

QMK is an open-source universal firmware for keyboards. Learn more about QMK in their docs.

Pros

  • Supports Windows, MacOS, and Linux
  • Can be configured using VIA, a web-based configurator. No need to do any scripting or coding.
  • Supports 4 layers (key-binding layouts)
  • Doesn't require any client app (like AHK) running in the background
  • Community support on QMK and VIA Discord
  • Can be set and used later on another device (config is written into the Lynepad)

Cons

  • More difficult AHK support out of the box
  • Color of the RGB Arc cannot be changed as it is used as layer indicator. You can turn it off or change the brightness.
  • Some special features such as RGB Arc following the thumwheel are missing

The QVEX firmware sends F22 keystroke together with A - U keystrokes by default. This can be detected by AHK client and translated to your desired actions. Or you can use the QVEX Desktop app (alpha version) to reconfigure the default keystrokes Lynepad is sending.

AHK Client (Recommended) QVEX Desktop app (Alpha)

Pros

  • Define unlimited number of layers (key-binding layouts)
  • Define separate layer for each app running on your PC (automatic switching using process name)
  • Supports complex content-aware macros

Cons

  • Windows only
  • Config is not written into the Lynepad, the keys are just interpreted by the AHK
  • Requires running AHK client
  • Configuration done through script writing

Pros

  • Setup Lynepad using nice graphical interface
  • Highest level of utility as this is a proprietery app
  • Easy firmware updates
  • Support basic macros
  • Define up to 4 layers
  • Can be set and used later on another device (config is written into the Lynepad)

Cons

  • Windows only (MacOS and Linux support will be added in the future) Currentely in alpha testing on our Discord
  • Very limited capabilities as QVEX Desktop is currentely an alpha version

docs.title: Flashing (uploading) a different firmware
docs.text:
Experimental QVEX Flasher

Use at your own risk. Do not use it without previous experience with flashing avr109 compatible boards.

We are working on QVEX Flasher, a web-based tool for reflashing the Lynepad firmware that works under chromium-based desktop browsers on all major operating systems.

After reflashing the firmware, perform a factory reset as there might be some leftover data stored on the EEPROM memory.

Windows & MacOSLinux
  1. Download .hex file of your desired firmware (QMK or QVEX Firmware)
  2. Download and run the QMK Toolbox.
  3. In case you haven't already done so when installing the QMK Toolbox, install the drivers by pressing Ctrl + N .
  4. Click the "Open" button and locate your downloaded firmware file.
  5. Plug in your Lynepad
  6. Check the auto-flash checkbox
  7. Reset the Lynepad with the provided SIM tool. The firmware should start uploading.
  1. Download .hex file of your desired firmware (QMK or QVEX Firmware)
  2. Plug in your Lynepad
  3. Run the script below
  4. Reset the device to using provided SIM tool while the script is running

This script is in works. It might not work as expected.

You need to have avrdude package installed on your system

fwupload.sh
#!/bin/bash

# Check if the .hex file argument is provided
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
echo "Please provide the path to the .hex file as an argument."
echo "Example: ./fwupload.sh my-hex-file.hex"
exit 1
fi

# Extract the .hex file path from the first argument
hex_file="$1"

# Set the initial state flag
initial_state="true"

# Continuously check for newly connected USB devices
while true; do
# Get the list of available devices
devices=$(ls /dev/tty*)

# Check if any new device is present
if [[ $devices != "$old_devices" && $initial_state != "true" ]]; then
# Iterate through the devices to find the target device
while read -r device; do
if [[ $device =~ "ttyACM" ]]; then
# Upload the .hex file to the device using avrdude
avrdude -p atmega32u4 -c avr109 -P "$device" -U flash:w:"$hex_file"

# Exit the loop after successful upload
break
fi
done <<< "$devices"
fi

# Save the current list of devices
old_devices="$devices"

# Set the initial state flag to false after the first run
initial_state="false"

# Wait for a few seconds before checking again
sleep 5
done

docs.title: QVEX Firmware
docs.text: To flash the QVEX Firmware on your Lynepad, please refer to the firmware overview page

docs.title: How the QVEX firmware works
docs.text:
Original compiled firmware can be found on the resources page

As components on the PCB are NOT specifially connected to the interrupt pins of the MCU, the firmware relies on scanning all the input components periodically (~200us) including the rotation of the encoders. Note that this may change in future revision

When there is action detected, the firmware presses F22 as a modifier and a key corresponding to the action.

F22 is used to prevent interfering with a regular computer keyboard or other accesorries. It is detected by AHK, and translated into user defined action.

Example: Left key in the top row is considered action a. When pressed, the firmware sends F22 + A key combination. AHK on the client computer detects the pressed combination that includes the F22 key and translates this into the user defined action.

docs.title: Switches
docs.text: The firmware uses matrix scanning to determine which of the keys are pressed.

docs.title: Encoders rotation
docs.text: Direction of the rotation is determined by which of the outputs from the encoder changes its state first.

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text: The core of a Lynepad is an AtMega32u4 MCU. Lynepad contains USB Type C, which can be also used for programming the MCU.

docs.title: Switches
docs.text: The PCB is designed to be used with any 3pin or 5pin TH MX compatible switches.

docs.title: Thumbwheel
docs.text:
Component: EC11 TH 9.5mm rotary encoder Outputs:

  • Direction
  • Push

Note: this maybe replaced with an SMD version of the same encoder in rev.2.

docs.title: Rotary joystick
docs.text:
Component: RKJXT1F42001 rotary encoder Outputs:

  • Direction
  • Push
  • Tilt (4way)

Slight tilt will trigger the tilt signal. Stronger tilt will however trigger both the tilt and the push signal. Slight tilt may be triggered by accident when trying to rotate or press the encoder.

docs.title: RGB LEDs
docs.text: Component: 4x WS2812B-2020

docs.title: License
docs.text: This manual is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.

docs.title: How to change the color of the LEDs?
docs.text:
QMK FirmwareQVEX Firmware

All the lightning options can be changed under the "lighting tab" in VIA configurator or using keys on layer #4. To do this, follow the instructions bellow.

Following instructions assume all the actions on layer #4 are set to their defaults.

Lynepad physical layout

A B C D E F G H I J K

Hold down the thumbwheel to activate layer #4. While holding the thumbwheel, you can use following keys:

  • E: Toggle backlighting
  • F: Change backlight intensity
  • Joystick rotation: Adjust RGB Arc intensity

Lynepad physical layout

A B C D E F G H I J K

Hold down the D and H key together. Then press the thumbwheel while pressing the keys. An animation should appear telling you that your entered the LED setting mode.

Later you can use A to exit the LED setting mode.

LED modes

  • I: Turn off LED arc
  • J: Arc LED Mode 1 - Solid light
  • K: Arc LED Mode 2 - The light will rotate with the thumbwheel.

Change color and brightness

  • C: Change backlighting brightness by rotating the joystick (rev. 2.x)
  • D: Change LED arc hue by rotating the joystick.
  • H: Change LED arc brightness by rotating the joystick.
  • G: Change LED arc saturation by rotating the joystick (Firmware version 1.0.4 or newer)

docs.title: Programming a Lynepad
docs.text:
A Lynepad (AtMega32u4 MCU) can be programmed through the USB Type C. We upload a caterina bootloader to the chip together with the firmware. This way you can also treat a Lynepad like an Arduino Leonardo in Arduino IDE.

Or you can use the ICSP connector onboard to program the MCU without using the bootloader.

You will need avrdude or Arduino IDE (which uses avrdude to upload the firmware).

With Arduino IDE you can easily both write and upload your code to your Lynepad. avrdude takes .hex file and uploads it to the MCU.

Warning for rev.1.3 or older: the method used to reset the MCU relies on the firmware providing the COM port. With another firmware, the COM port might not be provided. In that case, you need to disassemble your unit and use the hardware reset button located in the bottom left corner of the PCB. Read more in the "Resetting the MCU" section.

docs.title: Reseting the MCU
docs.text:
When uploading to a Lynepad through the USB, the MCU needs to be resetted prior to upload.

Rev. 2.0 or newer: Use the provided SIM tool to press the reset button located in the back of the Lynepad.

Rev. 1.3 or older: Reset can be achieved by opening and closing serial connection with a speed of 1200bps. This will connect another virtual COM port for a limited time, which can be used to program the MCU.

docs.title: Upload
docs.text:
When the MCU is ready, you can start the upload.

Example command: avrdude -p atmega32u4 -v -c avr109 -P COM21 -b 57600 -U flash:w:firmware.hex:a

avrdude options

docs.title: Arduino IDE
docs.text:
Installing the QVEX Tech board package to Arduino IDE:

  1. go to File -> Preferences and add following link to "Additional Boards Managers URLs". https://qvex.eu/files/package_qvex_index.json
  2. go to Tools -> Board -> Board Manager : here search for qvex package and hit install
  3. just select Lynepad under Tools -> Board, and you're ready to go

Alternatively you can treat the Lynepad as Arduino Leonardo.

docs.title: Upload using ISP (ICSP connector)
docs.text:
Use an AVR programmer of your choice compatible with avrdude or Arduino IDE (we use USBasp H6).

avrdude docs

docs.title: Restore default config
docs.text:
Note

This will not restore Lynepad to it's original firmware, in case you have flashed the alternative. This will only restore the default configuration of currentely flashed firmware.

QMK FirmwareQVEX Firmware

Following instructions assume all the actions on layer #4 are set to their defaults. If not, you can assign the EE_CLR keycode to a physical key using "Any" key function in VIA, and pressing the physical key.

Lynepad physical layout

A B C D E F G H I J K

  1. Hold down the thumbwheel to activate layer #4.
  2. While holding the thumbwheel, press the J key.
  3. This will resotre the QMK to it's default state.
  1. Unplug the Lynepad
  2. While holding the thumwheel, plug the Lynepad back in.
  3. Two green flashes should appear as confirmation of a successful reset.

docs.title: Getting started - AHK
docs.text:
What is AHK?

AutoHotkey is an interpreter for .ahk scripts, which can be used to perform action upon detecting input from the Lynepad.

This guide and the example AHK scripts assume your Lynepad is running QVEX Firmware.

AHK only supports Windows. For MacOS and Linux users, please refer to the Firmware overview page to switch to the QMK firmware.

docs.title: 2. Install AHK
docs.text: AHK Client

docs.title: 3. Download sample .ahk script
docs.text:
Sample .ahk

The script tells Lynepad (AHK) what action to perform. It can press key combinations on your keyboard, rotate mousewheel, open apps, and so much more.

More about the prepared .ahk script here.

docs.title: 4. Edit and/or run the script
docs.text:
By default, lynepad can only work in KiCad.

To add layout for another software, edit the .ahk script in a text editor of your choice. Further instructions are located inside the script.

When ready, just double click the script to activate it.

More about the prepared .ahk script here.

docs.title: 5. Done!
docs.text:
Lynepad is ready to go! Here are some things you may find useful:

How to change color and brightness of the LEDs?

docs.title: Prepared .ahk script
docs.text:
We prepared .ahk file which includes layout for KiCad.

Everything you need to customize and use the script is explained inside.

We recommend putting the script into the startup folder so it starts automatically with Windows. To open the folder press Win+R and enter shell:startup

docs.title: QVEX Lynepad quickstart guide
docs.text:
This page will guide you through the setup and basic usage of a Lynepad.

Are you running an older Lynepad version?

Up until order #00006 (including), the preloaded firmware was the QVEX firmware. If that's your case or your Lynepad is running QVEX Firmware for other reasons, you can continue to a guide on how to setup Lynepad running QVEX Firmware, or reflash the firmware with the QMK according to the Firmware overview page.

docs.title: 2. Configuration
docs.text:
1. Open VIA configurator
  2. Click "Authorize device" and select Lynepad
  3. Configure the Lynepad as you wish :))
VIA tutorial

For better understanding of the VIA configurator, we recommend a video tutorial by Austin V.

docs.title: 3. Done!
docs.text:
Here are some things you might find useful:

Full list of supported keycodes

How to change LED brighntess and color?

How to reset firmware to it's default?

docs.title: Alternative firmware
docs.text:
What is firmware?

Firmware is the program that runs inside of the Lynepad. It is responsible for detecting key-presses, and communicating with the connected computer. Different firmwares can behave differently, and support communication with a different software on your computer. You can replace the firmware by uploading (flashing) another.

The preloaded firmware is (starting from order number #00007) the QMK. We are developing our own QVEX Firmware together with our QVEX Desktop app. There might be reasons for you to switch to the official QVEX Firmware such as easier AHK support. You can learn more about available firmwares, compare them, and learn how to upload a different firmware on the Firmware overview page.

docs.title: Setup QVEX Firmware
docs.text:
AHKQVEX Desktop (Alpha)

AHK setup page

On our Discord under QVEX Desktop app category, select the Releases channel. Here you will find QVEX Desktop app available for download. If you are on QMK, have an older version of Lynepad, or you are on an older firmware version, QVEX Desktop app will detect your Lynepad and offer you the firmware update (reflash).

Danger

Please carefully read through all the warnings and notes on the Discord. The QVEX Desktop app has very limited functionality as it is in alpha version.
